items.q: Did Abraham follow the Torah's laws as Jews do today?
items.correct: No - Abraham lived long before the Torah was given at Sinai; tradition honours him as the first to turn from idolatry to one God and as father of the covenant people.
items.misconception: Abraham kept kosher laws and festivals exactly like modern Jews.
items.random_wrong: Abraham was a king who ruled from Jerusalem.
items.why: Students flatten the timeline; distinguishing the patriarchal covenant from the later Sinai covenant is basic to the narrative.

items.q: Did Moses lead the people into the Promised Land?
items.correct: No - according to the Torah, Moses saw the land from Mount Nebo but died before entering it; Joshua led the Israelites across the Jordan.
items.misconception: Moses personally led the conquest and settlement of Canaan.
items.random_wrong: Moses sailed the people to Canaan by ship.
items.why: The poignant ending of Deuteronomy is regularly missed; the Moses/Joshua handover is a standard exam detail.

items.q: Is the covenant just a one-sided promise from God?
items.correct: No - the covenant is a two-way bond: God's promises of land and descendants come with obligations, such as circumcision for Abraham and Torah observance at Sinai.
items.misconception: The covenant only involves God giving and Israel receiving.
items.random_wrong: The covenant was an agreement between Abraham and Pharaoh.
items.why: The mutual structure of covenant explains mitzvot and Jewish practice; one-sided readings make later obligations inexplicable.

items.q: What was the sign of God's covenant with Abraham?
items.why: It's Circumcision (brit milah). “The rainbow” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which patriarch was given the new name 'Israel'?
items.why: Correct answer: Jacob. “Abraham” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How was Moses called by God to lead the Israelites?
items.misconception: In a dream about seven cows
items.random_wrong: By a voice from the Ark
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Through the burning bush. “In a dream about seven cows” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: The Israelites' deliverance from slavery in Egypt
items.misconception: The giving of the Ten Commandments
items.why: It's The Israelites' deliverance from slavery in Egypt. “The giving of the Ten Commandments” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many sons of Jacob became the ancestors of the tribes of Israel?
items.why: The real figure is 12 sons. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
